seoTitle: Remove Duplicate Bank Feed Transactions in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Clean up overlapping bank feed entries in QuickBooks Online caused by sync glitches or overlapping CSV uploads.
intro: Sync glitches or overlapping CSV uploads can leave duplicate charges sitting in your bank feed. This guide shows how to spot and safely remove duplicate transactions in QuickBooks Online without disrupting your reconciliations.

steps.heading: Open your bank feed
steps.body: Go to Transactions, then Bank Transactions, and select the account with suspected duplicates.

steps.heading: Identify duplicates
steps.body: Compare dates, merchant descriptions, and exact amounts across rows to spot overlapping entries.

steps.heading: Select and exclude
steps.body: Check the boxes next to the extra rows, then click Exclude in the floating action banner to pull them out of your active feed.

steps.heading: Review the Excluded tab
steps.body: Switch to the Excluded tab to confirm the duplicate rows landed there safely.

steps.heading: Permanently delete
steps.body: Select the rows you're confident are duplicates and click Delete in the batch toolbar to purge them from your system.

faq.question: Why do duplicate transactions show up in my bank feed?
faq.answer: They typically happen when automatic feed loops glitch or when manual CSV uploads overlap with an active direct bank connection.

faq.question: Do I delete duplicates directly, or exclude them first?
faq.answer: You should exclude them first, which moves them to a safety tab, then permanently delete from there once you're confident they're duplicates.

faq.question: Can I recover a transaction I accidentally excluded?
faq.answer: Yes, use the Undo option on the excluded row to bring it back into your active feed if it was actually a real transaction.

faq.question: How do duplicates affect my books if left unresolved?
faq.answer: They can distort income statements and cause major balance variances during month-end bank reconciliations.
